description: Add a reflected lens-view op so the CLI can project any config-declared lens as a binned item-view — closes FGAP-073 at the general (all-lenses) altitude. A new op (proposed canonical name context-lens-view, sibling to context-edges-for-lens; confirm at plan time) keyed by --lensId reuses the shipped primitives loadLensView (packages/pi-context/src/lens-view.ts:43) + renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105) to return/render a lens's target items grouped into its declared bins. ADDITIVE per the FEAT-008 invariant: a NEW op reusing existing primitives; context-edges-for-lens + every other in-pi op byte-unchanged. The new op is slotted in the CLI grouped-help classifier (groupForOp in cli.ts — the exhaustive-disjoint guard forces this) and routed through the boundary read-cap (structureForRead) so a large view fail-closes. Implement via the canonical pipeline (explore -> plan -> agent -> verify, iterate to zero).
acceptance_criteria: CLI-PROVABLE (the originating friction): `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId gaps-by-status` (text) renders framework-gaps grouped under its declared bin headings (identified / accepted / in-progress / closed / wontfix), each gap a line carrying id + status + title — the binned VIEW, not edges. Running this answers 'see open gaps' directly.

CLI-PROVABLE: `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId gaps-by-status --json` returns a single-parse structured object grouping items into bins (bin -> item records with id/status/title), distinct from context-edges-for-lens's flat Edge[]; the same payload is produced by --format json.

CLI-PROVABLE (lens-generic, not lensId-specific — the FGAP-073 general class): running `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId <id>` for EACH of the four declared lenses (gaps-by-status, gaps-by-priority, tasks-by-status, features-by-status) returns that lens's target block binned by its declared field; an op-level test additionally exercises kind=composition + hand-curated dispatch through the shared loadLensView primitive.
CLI-PROVABLE (fails clean): `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId does-not-exist` emits a field-named error and a non-zero classifier exit code (not a crash, not exit 0).

CLI-PROVABLE (read-cap honored): a lens view whose serialization exceeds the 50KB boundary cap fail-closes like every other read op (over-cap node -> data:null / complete:false / truncated:true) rather than dumping unbounded or crashing — exercise on gaps-by-status (framework-gaps already exceeds 50KB flat).

CLI-PROVABLE (discoverability auto-covered): `pi-context context-lens-view --help` renders the best-of-breed per-op template (SYNOPSIS + typed flags + EXAMPLES + RELATED + footer) and `--help --format json` emits the structured help model; the op carries an authored examples[] (the gaps-by-status invocation); the op appears in grouped `pi-context --help` under its derived group (slotted in groupForOp; the exhaustive-disjoint guard stays green).

Additive + gates: in-pi ops byte-unchanged except the new op; `npx tsx scripts/parity-check.ts` exit 0 (the op<->CLI parity gate auto-covers the new op); `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` fail 0 with new op-level tests (per-lens binning correctness, the cap fail-close, the unknown-lens error).

Closure: FGAP-073 closed by this task (verification_verifies_item + complete-task); canonical pipeline green — the runtime demo IS the CLI commands in the criteria above, plus a fresh adversarial probe re-deriving lens-generality + the cap fail-close + additivity.
notes: Addresses FGAP-073 (general altitude: covers all four target-lenses + composition/hand-curated, gaps-by-status the trigger). Reuses loadLensView (lens-view.ts:43) + renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105) — exists-but-unexposed, so additive reflection not new machinery. Adjacent to FEAT-004 (config-driven state-derivation) but distinct (lens projection). docs-surface-sync: the new op's description/promptSnippet + examples[] are user-facing surface; README/SKILL audit + CHANGELOG [Unreleased].
